REPO: quarkusio/quarkus
PR #53038: Agents: Add AGENTS.md and coding skills for the Quarkus codebase
Changed files (11): .agents/skills/building-and-testing/SKILL.md, .agents/skills/classloading-and-runtime-dev/SKILL.md, .agents/skills/coding-style/SKILL.md, .agents/skills/creating-extensions/SKILL.md, .agents/skills/working-with-config/SKILL.md, .agents/skills/writing-build-steps/SKILL.md, .agents/skills/writing-tests/SKILL.md, .github/workflows/ci-actions-incremental.yml, .gitignore, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md
Diff (+790/-3):
PR DESCRIPTION:
## Summary

Add `AGENTS.md` and `CLAUDE.md` with detailed coding skill files in `.agents/skills/` to guide coding agents working on the Quarkus codebase.

- **`AGENTS.md`** — Essential conventions (project structure, classloading rules, build commands, coding style). Supported directly by most coding tools (Cursor, Continue, Cline, Goose, Windsurf, GitHub Copilot).
- **`CLAUDE.md`** — References `AGENTS.md` and lists the on-demand skills for Claude Code.
- **`.agents/skills/`** — 7 detailed skill files:
  - `writing-build-steps` — `@BuildStep` methods, build items, recorders
  - `writing-tests` — `QuarkusExtensionTest` patterns, test locations, native test registration
  - `working-with-config` — `@ConfigMapping` interfaces, config phases, annotation processor
  - `classloading-and-runtime-dev` — Split classloading model, runtime-dev modules
  - `creating-extensions` — Extension module layout and creation checklist
  - `coding-style` — Formatting, visibility, naming, logging conventions
  - `building-and-testing` — Maven commands, flags, incremental builds

No tool-specific instruction files (`.cursor/`, `.continue/`, `.clinerules/`, `.goosehints`, `.windsurfrules`, `.github/copilot-instructions.md`) — all these tools now support `AGENTS.md` directly.

DIFF:

--- .agents/skills/building-and-testing/SKILL.md (+84/-0) ---
@@ -0,0 +1,84 @@
+---
+name: building-and-testing
+description: >
+  How to build and test Quarkus: Maven commands, build flags, incremental
+  builds, justfile aliases, and important build rules.
+---
+
+# Build System
+
+Quarkus uses Maven. The full build is large; know how to build selectively.
+Ensure `MAVEN_OPTS` includes at least `-Xmx4g` for builds (do not override the user's existing `MAVEN_OPTS`).
+
+## Quick Build (First Time)
+
+```bash
+./mvnw -Dquickly
+```
+
+This skips tests, ITs, docs, native, and validation.
+
+## Building a Specific Extension
+
+```bash
+# All modules of an extension
+./mvnw install -f extensions/<name>/
+
+# A single module
+./mvnw install -f extensions/<name>/deployment
+```
+
+## Building Core
+
+```bash
+./mvnw install -f core/ -DskipTests
+```
+
+## Running Tests
+
+```bash
+# Run tests for an extension
+./mvnw verify -f extensions/<name>/
+
+# Run a single test class
+./mvnw test -f integration-tests/<name>/ -Dtest=MyTest
+
+# Run a single test method
+./mvnw verify -Dtest=fully.qualified.ClassName#methodName
+
+# Native integration tests
+./mvnw verify -f integration-tests/<name>/ -Dnative
+```
+
+## Incremental Build
+
+```bash
+# Build only changed modules and their downstream dependencies
+./mvnw install -Dincremental
+```
+
+## Key Maven Flags
+
+| Flag | Purpose |
+|------|---------|
+| `-Dquickly` | Skip tests, ITs, docs, native, validation |
+| `-DquicklyDocs` | Like `-Dquickly` but includes docs |
+| `-Dnative` | Build and test native image |
+| `-Dno-format` | Skip code formatting check |
+| `-DskipTests` / `-DskipITs` | Skip unit / integration tests |
+| `-Dno-test-modules` | Skip building test modules |
+| `-Dincremental` | Only build changed modules |
+
+## Justfile
+
+A justfile (`.justfile` in the repo root) provides convenience aliases. Run `just -l` to
+see them. By default uses `./mvnw -T0.8C` (parallel build). Set `QMVNCMD`
+to override the Maven command (e.g., `QMVNCMD=mvnd` for Maven Daemon).
+
+## Important Build Rules
+
+- Always `install` (not just `compile`) when building modules that other modules depend on
+- If you change a runtime module, rebuild its deployment module too
+- If you change something in `core/`, downstream extensions may need rebuilding
+- Use `./mvnw` (the wrapper) to ensure the correct Maven version
+- After adding/removing/renaming an extension, run `update-extension-dependencies.sh`
--- .agents/skills/classloading-and-runtime-dev/SKILL.md (+80/-0) ---
@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+---
+name: classloading-and-runtime-dev
+description: >
+  Quarkus split classloading model, runtime-dev module wiring, conditional
+  dependencies, and common classloading mistakes.
+---
+
+# Classloading and Runtime-Dev Modules
+
+## The Two ClassLoaders
+
+1. **Deployment ClassLoader** — Used at build time. Has access to all
+   dependencies including deployment-only libraries.
+2. **Runtime ClassLoader** — Used at application runtime. Only has access to
+   runtime dependencies.
+
+## Core Rules
+
+- **Runtime code MUST NOT reference deployment classes.** Violations cause
+  `ClassNotFoundException` at runtime.
+- **Deployment code CAN reference runtime classes.**
+- **Recorders bridge the gap.** A `@Recorder` lives in the runtime module but
+  is invoked from deployment build steps.
+
+## Runtime-Dev Modules
+
+Extensions providing dev-mode-only functionality (e.g., Dev UI pages) use a
+`runtime-dev/` module. These classes are **only** loaded in dev mode, not in
+production. This requires two steps:
+
+### 1. Register as conditional dev dependency
+
+In the **runtime** module's `pom.xml`, configure `quarkus-extension-maven-plugin`:
+
+```xml
+<plugin>
+    <groupId>io.quarkus</groupId>
+    <artifactId>quarkus-extension-maven-plugin</artifactId>
+    <executions>
+        <execution>
+            <phase>process-resources</phase>
+            <goals>
+                <goal>extension-descriptor</goal>
+            </goals>
+            <configuration>
+                <conditionalDevDependencies>
+                    <artifact>${project.groupId}:${project.artifactId}-dev:${project.version}</artifact>
+                </conditionalDevDependencies>
+            </configuration>
+        </execution>
+    </executions>
+</plugin>
+```
+
+**Without this, runtime-dev classes will NOT be loaded in dev mode** — you get
+`ClassNotFoundException`.
+
+### 2. Guard build steps with IsLocalDevelopment
+
+```java
+@BuildSteps(onlyIf = IsLocalDevelopment.class)
+class MyExtensionDevUIProcessor {
+    // Dev UI build steps here
+}
+```
+
+## Why This Matters
+
+Forgetting `conditionalDevDependencies` is particularly confusing because:
+- The code compiles without errors
+- It only fails at runtime in dev mode
+- The error message doesn't hint at the missing configuration
+
+## Common Mistakes
+
+- Importing a deployment class in runtime code
+- Using a build-time-only library in a recorder
+- Accessing runtime config during augmentation (build time)
+- Putting user-facing API classes in deployment instead of runtime
+- Forgetting `conditionalDevDependencies` for `runtime-dev` mod
reference_review:
POINTS:
- [.agents/skills/classloading-and-runtime-dev/SKILL.md:23] I don't know if these are specific enough for an agent. Also, the idea of having a single "deployment class loader" and a single "runtime class loader" is not really guaranteed to remain true even through the end of this year, and in my mind recorders are already obsolete (OK, so the actual code has to catch up to my imagination, but it will soon-ish hopefully); what is the effect of "poisoning" AI with old knowledge? Will we have a hard time retraining it?

Can we instead stick to concepts that are more likely to remain future-proof, and say something along the lines of "Deployment Layer" and "Runtime Layer" and not reference the word "ClassLoader"? And maybe not talk about recorders in such a general way at all (the idea of AI writing recorders fills me with dread)?
- [.agents/skills/classloading-and-runtime-dev/SKILL.md:65] Are we *certain* we want AIs to be using `onlyIf`? It's already going to be really hard to get humans to stop doing it. We're digging the hole faster than it can be filled.
- [.agents/skills/coding-style/SKILL.md:27] If we update the ADR, will we have a problem with AIs that have old knowledge?
- [.agents/skills/creating-extensions/SKILL.md:8] Same question about ADR updating applies to this entire section. Can we be sure that it is possible to un-poison the AI if it has wrong or outdated info?
- [.agents/skills/working-with-config/SKILL.md:31] Can we add a section that explicitly states that we should not use `Optional` for the purpose of defining a default value?

e.g. bad:

```java
String name = cfg.optName().orElse("theDefaultName"); // <- this is bad, don't do it
```

Even if the default is dynamic! We don't have a centralized solution yet but there are ways to do it.
- [.agents/skills/writing-build-steps/SKILL.md:42] Can we update this section to say that build steps should only ever produce one item whenever possible? Producing multiple items is a sign that something is wrong... yes I know we do it a lot but again we don't want to dig the hole faster than we're filling it!
- [.agents/skills/writing-tests/SKILL.md:18] I don't feel good that we're telling AIs to use these without having a detailed technical specification of how these will behave. Like: *how* are we starting a Quarkus app with `@QuarkusTest`? Are there assumptions that should or should not be made with respect to class loading for example? What happens when the tests complete? Things like that.

Diff (+1039/-25):
PR DESCRIPTION:
Implementation of what's being discussed on:
 - [#dev > JVM parameters recommender](https://quarkusio.zulipchat.com/#narrow/channel/187038-dev/topic/JVM.20parameters.20recommender/with/538027156)

Fixes #51434 

(This was a draft, updating description)

This is part of a larger plan described on https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/discussions/51041 but can be merged independently.

It should be followed up some further polishing to be tracked on https://github.com/orgs/quarkusio/projects/59/views/1  - but I consider this a good incremental improvement which doesn't strictly require any follow-up.

Most notably this will avoid all warnings related to module usage, and reconfigure the module system fully transparently w/o users needing to apply any changes _in most cases_. When we can't fully automate something, an actionable warning should be visible.

reference_review:
SUMMARY:
I think the build item looks good for the most part. I have some questions and comments inline.

POINTS:
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/jvm/ResolvedJVMRequirements.java:22] Why do we need an aggregated item for this? If new packaging types need to add support for these options, would they not have to add more methods to this? IMO the code to do this belongs with the packaging type.

Shared static utilities to construct the individual option sequences for JARs and JVMs would be more generally useful; locking these methods behind an instance creates a barrier that isn't really necessary IMO.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/pkg/jar/AbstractJarBuilder.java:219] I guess we'd just repeat this for every new kind of manifest entry? Maybe it'd make more sense to just consume the add-opens items directly?
- [extensions/netty/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/netty/deployment/NettyOnJava24ProcessorExtension.java:28] Why would the target module not be e.g. `io.netty.transport.classes.epoll` or whatever actually needs it?
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/builditem/ModuleOpenBuildItem.java:57] I maintain that the `openingModuleName` should be forbidden from being `ALL-UNNAMED`. Otherwise we'll have to redo 100% of this work once we're modular in any way, because we won't have any unnamed modules. Whereas in the non-modular mode, we can easily consolidate the named modules into `ALL-UNNAMED` using a few stream operations. We could provide a reusable utility method to do this.

By requiring each consumer to give the opening module name explicitly, we improve self-documentation at the least. In the worst case, if one or two of these ends up being wrong once modularization happens, that's fine, and easier to fix than having to redo all of them because everyone chose the path of least resistance and gave `ALL-UNNAMED` (which they absolutely will do).
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/builditem/ModuleOpenBuildItem.java:42] We have `Assert.checkNotEmptyParam(...)` in `smallrye-common`. We also have `Assert.checkNotNullParam(...)` if you want to be consistent there.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/jvm/ResolvedJVMRequirements.java:50] Should this be a warning though? Maybe it'd be better to merge the options in this case.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/jvm/ReflectiveAccessModulesReconfigurer.java:100] See https://word-bits.flurg.com/posts/the-new-reflection-basics/#handling-exceptions for the pattern that should be used here.

Unrelatedly, I maintain that `jdk.internal.module.Modules` is the best API to use for this.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/JBossThreadsProcessor.java:19] ```suggestion
        return new ModuleOpenBuildItem("java.base", "org.jboss.threads", "java.lang");
```
- [integration-tests/test-extension/extension/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/extest/deployment/ModulesCustomProcessor.java:10] What is requiring this? Is this just for a test to consume?

POINTS:
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/JniProcessor.java:34] This changes the default value from `true` to `false`; we don't want that! I'd suggest to leave this one alone; it was correct already.
- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/deployment/pkg/NativeConfig.java:42] Same here.  Only remove the default values if they're `false` or `0`.
- [core/test-extension/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/extest/runtime/config/AllValuesConfig.java:33] This should not be changed: it is like this deliberately in order to test `Optional`.  If we change it to `OptionalLong` then we won't be testing that anymore.
- [extensions/flyway/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/flyway/runtime/FlywayDataSourceBuildTimeConfig.java:34] This one shouldn't change to an array (almost every usage of this property treats it as a list).  And, it wasn't `Optional` before so it should not become `Optional` now.
- [extensions/hibernate-orm/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/hibernate/orm/deployment/HibernateOrmProcessor.java:639] Maybe instead go with the original but slightly modified:
```suggestion
                hibernateConfig.jdbc.statementFetchSize.ifPresent(
                        fetchSize -> desc.getProperties().setProperty(AvailableSettings.STATEMENT_FETCH_SIZE,
                                Integer.toString(fetchSize));
```
Smaller diffs are usually preferred.
- [extensions/hibernate-orm/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkus/hibernate/orm/deployment/HibernateOrmProcessor.java:644] Same here.
- [extensions/vertx-http/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/vertx/http/runtime/ServerSslConfig.java:25] This one doesn't need to change: it's being used as a list, not an array.
- [extensions/vertx-http/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/vertx/http/runtime/cors/CORSConfig.java:34] These ones don't need to change, since they're being used as lists not arrays.
- [extensions/vertx-http/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/vertx/http/runtime/cors/CORSConfig.java:45] These ones don't need to change, since they're being used as lists not arrays.
- [extensions/vertx-http/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkus/vertx/http/runtime/cors/CORSConfig.java:55] These ones don't need to change, since they're being used as lists not arrays.

SUMMARY:
Just some minor comments about the code and we can merge.

POINTS:
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/debug/HTMLLaunchableAdapterFactory.java:34] You don't need to repeat the condition on adapterType here.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/debug/HTMLLaunchableAdapterFactory.java:32] The `else` here can be removed (`else`s after a `return` are useless).
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/debug/IsLaunchableHTMLTester.java:29] parenthesis here are not necessay.

SUMMARY:
See some comments inline.
The .css file of the angular-app could probably be removed as well.

POINTS:
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/META-INF/MANIFEST.MF:5] As it's not changed, this needs to stay to 0.1.0
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.xml/pom.xml:10] As it's not changed, this needs to stay to 0.1.0
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.tests/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/tests/TestLanguageServers.java:280] Does that line change anything? It's setting document with same contents as previously. Is it "only" to trigger an event?
If so, maybe it'd be best if you start from a clean file, check there are not markers and then make a modification on the document and see that markers are added accordingly.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.tests/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/tests/TestLanguageServers.java:297] Would be good to check also for a keyword like "Component" in the marker message to ensure the message comes from the right LS.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.tests/testProjects/angular-app/.gitignore:1] I think you can remove this file.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.tests/testProjects/angular-app/angular.json:58] I think you can remove this goals and next ones and keep only `build` (you may even be able to remove build)
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.tests/testProjects/angular-app/angular.json:106] You can probably fully remove this `project`
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.tests/testProjects/angular-app/package.json:27] You may be able to remove all the devDependencies.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.tests/testProjects/angular-app/src/app/app.component.spec.ts:1] You may be able to remove this test file.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.tests/testProjects/angular-app/tslint.json:1] I think you can remove that file.

SUMMARY:
While the change overall is interesting (but would be better happening in the LS), I don't think we should make the catalog load a blocker here.
Moreover, the catalog should probably be cached locally as the "current" value once it's fetched, so it would remain reusable without connection. And before fetching the whole catalog, it'd be nice to just get the HEAD and see if there are actual change to fetch since last change. I guess storing the last change date would be useful too.
For storing, you can use the preference store (just because it's the most convenient).

POINTS:
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper.tests/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/tests/TestLanguageServers.java:178] The fact that you need to grow the timeout here shows that pulling the catalog should be happening asynchronously IMHO.
- [org.eclipse.wildwebdeveloper/src/org/eclipse/wildwebdeveloper/json/schema/Tuple.java:3] You may prefer to import org.eclipse.xtext.xbase.lib (that's already a transitive dependency) and use its Pair class.

SUMMARY:
There's still a need for `PortManager` in cases where ephemeral ports (`0`) can't be used. Where they can, ephemeral is obviously preferred.

To avoid collisions for assigned ports across multiple processes, `PortManager` should allocate from outside the ephemeral range. On Linux the default emphemeral range is 32768–60999:

    ❯ sysctl net.ipv4.ip_local_port_range
    net.ipv4.ip_local_port_range = 32768    60999

Proposal: reserve a block per JVM starting at a fixed offset. Each instance claims block `n` by binding a server socket on `20000 + (n * 1000)`, giving it 999 usable ports in that range. That handles coordination across multiple test JVMs without a shared registry. If ports run out, a new block could be allocated.

On release, `PortManager` should verify the port is actually free before returning it to the pool — sockets can linger in `TIME_WAIT` after close, so the free-list update needs to handle async release rather than marking the port available immediately.

POINTS:
- [pulsar-broker/src/test/java/org/apache/pulsar/client/impl/ServiceUrlQuarantineTest.java:109] Keeping the existing PortManager would be useful for this type of use cases. After closing the socket, the previous port could get reused immediately since the socket is no longer open.
- [pulsar-broker/src/test/java/org/apache/pulsar/broker/loadbalance/extensions/ExtensibleLoadManagerImplWithAdvertisedListenersTest.java:74] Keeping the existing PortManager would be useful for this type of use cases. After closing the socket, the previous port could get reused immediately since the socket is no longer open.
- [pulsar-broker/src/test/java/org/apache/pulsar/broker/loadbalance/impl/ModularLoadManagerImplTest.java:955] Keeping the existing PortManager would be useful for this type of use cases. After closing the socket, the previous port could get reused immediately since the socket is no longer open.

Diff (+350/-22):
PR DESCRIPTION:
## Summary
- TestRestTemplate previously disabled cookies by default (`StandardCookieSpec.IGNORE`), differing from RestTemplate which uses the HTTP client library's default
- Aligns the defaults so TestRestTemplate no longer explicitly ignores cookies
- Adds `withCookies(HttpCookies)` method for explicit cookie control, following the same pattern as `withRedirects(HttpRedirects)` from #43431
- Introduces `HttpCookies` enum, `HttpComponentsCookieSpec` adapter, and `cookies()` on `RestTemplateBuilder`
- Deprecates `HttpClientOption.ENABLE_COOKIES`

Fixes gh-48607

## Test plan
- [ ] `TestRestTemplateTests` — verifies default cookie spec is now null (library default), `withCookies(ENABLE)` returns `"strict"`, `withCookies(DISABLE)` returns `"ignoreCookies"`
- [ ] `HttpClientSettingsTests` — verifies `withCookies()` and updated `orElse()` with cookies field
- [ ] `HttpClientAutoConfigurationTests` — verifies settings construction with new record shape
- [ ] Existing redirect tests remain green

reference_review:
SUMMARY:
Thanks for the PR, @apoorvdarshan. In addition to the two comments I've made, I think some changes to the various other `…HttpClientBuilder` classes are missing. The required changes will be similar to the changes that you've made for `HttpComponentsHttpClientBuilder` to honour the cookies configuration.

POINTS:
- [module/spring-boot-resttestclient/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/resttestclient/TestRestTemplate.java:1055] This should be "for removal in 4.3.0".
- [module/spring-boot-http-client/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/http/client/HttpClientSettings.java:39] This is a breaking API change as the four-argument constructor now no longer exists. To preserve compatibility, it should be declared explicitly in deprecated form.

SUMMARY:
Thanks very much, @scordio.

I've left a couple of very minor comments. No need for you to do anything about them unless you really want to. We can take care of them when we merge this. That'll take a little while as it'll hopefully go in Spring Boot 4.1 and we need to first get 4.0 out of the door and then create the 4.0.x maintenance branch.

POINTS:
- [system-test/spring-boot-image-system-tests/src/systemTest/java/org/springframework/boot/image/assertions/ContainerConfigAssert.java:93] This should remain as `See`.
- [system-test/spring-boot-image-system-tests/src/systemTest/java/org/springframework/boot/image/assertions/ContainerConfigAssert.java:128] This should remain as `See`.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/docs/howto/dataaccess/configuretwodatasources/MyAdditionalDataSourceConfiguration.java:33] The return type needs to be changed to `HikariDataSource` as well.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/docs/howto/dataaccess/configuretwodatasources/MyCompleteAdditionalDataSourceConfiguration.java:40] The return type needs to be changed to `HikariDataSource` as well.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/docs/howto/testing/slicetests/MyConfiguration.java:39] The return type needs to be changed to `HikariDataSource` as well.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/docs/howto/testing/slicetests/MyDatasourceConfiguration.java:31] The return type needs to be changed to `HikariDataSource` as well.

SUMMARY:
Thanks for the updates. I've left a couple of comments on one of the bootstrappers, but I think they apply to all of them. Would you like to take another look?

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-test-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/test/autoconfigure/jdbc/JdbcTestContextBootstrapper.java:37] This method can be removed now, can't it?
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-test-autoconfigure/src/main/java/org/springframework/boot/test/autoconfigure/jdbc/JdbcTestContextBootstrapper.java:54] This method can be removed now, can't it?

PR DESCRIPTION:
Fix typos in method names and javadoc; update the documentation to ensure it is more understandable and coherent.

<!--
Thanks for contributing to Spring Boot. Please review the following notes before
submitting a pull request.

Please submit only genuine pull-requests. Do not use this repository as a GitHub
playground.

Security Vulnerabilities

STOP! If your contribution fixes a security vulnerability, please do not submit it.
Instead, please head over to https://spring.io/security-policy to learn how to disclose a
vulnerability responsibly.

Dependency Upgrades

Please do not open a pull request for a straightforward dependency upgrade (one that
only updates the version property). We have a semi-automated process for such upgrades
that we prefer to use. However, if the upgrade is more involved (such as requiring
changes for removed or deprecated API) your pull request is most welcome.

Describing Your Changes

If, having reviewed the notes above, you're ready to submit your pull request, please
provide a brief description of the proposed changes. If they fix a bug, please
describe the broken behaviour and how the changes fix it. If they make an enhancement,
please describe the new functionality and why you believe it's useful. If your pull
request relates to any existing issues, please reference them by using the issue number
prefixed with #.
-->

 The Reactive Relational Database Connectivity (https://r2dbc.io[R2DBC]) project brings reactive programming APIs to relational databases.

reference_review:
SUMMARY:
Thanks very much, @Buzzardo. I've left some comments where I think there may be a better alternative, mostly instead of "through". Please let me know what you think when you have a minute.

POINTS:
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/actuator/endpoints.adoc:10] Could we use "over" here? I think it's more idiomatic when talking about a protocol.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/actuator/endpoints.adoc:463] over the web
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/actuator/endpoints.adoc:464] over JMX
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/actuator/endpoints.adoc:497] over JMX or HTTP
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/data/sql.adoc:428] using
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/documentation/container-images.adoc:7] I think with or using would read better here
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/deployment/cloud.adoc:125] Using, perhaps
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/documentation/messaging.adoc:5] over
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/features/external-config.adoc:725] I think either "by" or "using" would read better here
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/features/logging.adoc:14] Perhaps "using" or "with" here
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/features/testing.adoc:444] as
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/howto/actuator.adoc:63] Perhaps "using" or "with" here.
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/howto/webserver.adoc:472] used by Micrometer to expose metrics
- [spring-boot-project/spring-boot-docs/src/docs/asciidoc/io/caching.adoc:6] Just "using", perhaps
